title: Restart Optisweep service after heartbeat timeout
operational_intent: restart optisweep service after heartbeat timeout

steps.instruction: Confirm no RMS alarms are active.
steps.validation_check: RMS alarm state is documented.
steps.expected_outcome: No RMS alarm explains the stoppage.
steps.escalation_condition: Escalate if a safety or hardware alarm is present.

steps.instruction: Confirm tipper heartbeat timeout is present.
steps.validation_check: Heartbeat timeout state is captured.
steps.expected_outcome: Heartbeat timeout matches the recurring failure signature.
steps.escalation_condition: Escalate if OT hardware alarms are present.

steps.validation_check: Safe state is confirmed.
steps.expected_outcome: System is safe for service recovery.
steps.escalation_condition: Escalate if safe state cannot be confirmed.

steps.validation_check: Service returns to running state.
steps.escalation_condition: Escalate if remote access is unavailable.

steps.validation_check: Heartbeat timeout clears.
steps.escalation_condition: Escalate if heartbeat does not recover.

steps.validation_check: AGV movement is observed.

validation_checks: RMS alarm state is documented.
Heartbeat timeout clears.
AGV movement is observed.
escalation_conditions: remote access unavailable
heartbeat does not recover after restart
OT hardware alarms present

evidence_refs.excerpt: Service restart restored heartbeat and AGV movement.

operational_intent: restart optisweep service after heartbeat timeout

steps.instruction: Confirm no RMS alarms are active.
steps.validation_check: RMS alarm state is documented.
steps.expected_outcome: No RMS alarm explains the stoppage.
steps.escalation_condition: Escalate if a safety or hardware alarm is present.

title: Proc 229374 01
operational_intent: Capture source-backed timeout evidence with timestamps, event IDs, categories, and error text so engineering can assess whether database timeouts contributed to a WCS service failure.

steps.validation_check: The chosen time anchor matches at least one visible WCSWebApplication timeout screenshot.
At least one captured image clearly shows WCSWebApplication and the repeated 1:48:37 AM entries.
The captured detail includes both Event ID 20102 and timeout-related DbCommand text.
The captured detail explicitly shows Event ID 10100 and timeout-expired language.
The summary contains Event IDs 20102 and/or 10100 with timestamps and artifact links, and does not mislabel OPC errors as database timeouts.

validation_checks: At least one screenshot or log view shows WCSWebApplication with timestamp 4/4/2026 1:48:37 AM
Event ID 20102 and/or 10100 details are preserved with timeout wording
escalation_conditions: If Event ID 10100/20102 timeout evidence is present during outage window
If timeout evidence is repeated across multiple screenshots but cause remains unclear
If support cannot distinguish database timeout evidence from OPC/application exceptions

title: Proc 229374 02
operational_intent: Collect fast site-side diagnostic evidence when remote access is blocked or delayed.

steps.validation_check: The symptom description distinguishes between full stop and stop at tilt trays/tipper handoff.
A screenshot is captured that shows RMS status during the outage window, even if no errors are present.
Site confirms beacon/alarm state and support records it as a diagnostic signal, not proof of health.
A specific mismatch is documented between HMI/AVEVA state and physical state.
The result of the pause/unpause attempt is explicitly recorded as worked or failed.
At least one affected tipper value and one comparison value are recorded.

validation_checks: RMS message panel status is captured
Operator station alarm/beacon state is confirmed
Heartbeat values are collected for at least the affected tipper path
escalation_conditions: If RMS shows no robot errors but AGVs still do not move
If AVEVA/HMI state conflicts with field reality
If heartbeat values are inconsistent or one tipper shows zero

title: Proc 229374 03
operational_intent: Restore diagnostic access without waiting for external VPN remediation.

steps.validation_check: The case record clearly shows that normal remote access is blocked by an external access issue.
A single target IP is selected and recorded before RDP is attempted.
Support confirms that the field PC is visible and can be used for the next diagnostic step.
Either the remote desktop is visible or the exact failure message is recorded.
Support confirms usable application/server visibility after the workaround.

validation_checks: Field PC visibility is established
RDP to 10.27.80.165 succeeds or failure is explicitly documented
Ignition or server desktop becomes visible
escalation_conditions: If VPN/Zscaler remains unavailable and production is impacted
If field PC is the only viable path to server visibility
If RDP succeeds but application credentials still fail

title: Proc 229374 04
operational_intent: Restore operations when service degradation is suspected and authorized personnel are available.

steps.validation_check: The restart performer and reason are documented before the action is taken.
The responder has active server access before attempting the restart.
The restart action is explicitly documented with performer and time.
At least one post-restart confirmation is captured from the responder or case owner.
Recovery status and root-cause uncertainty are both documented.

validation_checks: Service restart action is recorded
Post-restart system is reported operational
Case notes show continued operation after restart
escalation_conditions: If restart restores service but cause remains unknown
If repeated restarts are needed
If authorized responder is not available to perform the restart

title: Proc 228086 01
operational_intent: Preserve screenshot-backed evidence showing memory trend, performance/status context, and any visible uptime or gateway state indicators without over-claiming crash confirmation.

steps.validation_check: Remote access is confirmed before attempting Ignition evidence capture.
Screenshot includes chart title and enough of the time axis to support later review.
Screenshot is legible enough to identify the page and preserve visible metrics for later review.
Conclusion does not overstate root cause and explicitly notes uncertainty.

validation_checks: Memory trend screenshot captured
Ignition status/performance page screenshot captured
Visible values preserved for memory, threads, CPU, or uptime if shown
escalation_conditions: If gateway status cannot be reached
If memory/status evidence is inconclusive and outage persists
If restart consideration requires higher-role approval

title: Proc 228086 02
operational_intent: Create a traceable visual evidence set for AGV movement failures and system error context.

steps.validation_check: Screenshot includes enough context to tie later detail screenshots to the same incident state.
At least one screenshot clearly shows the error panel names and associated entries.
Raw wording is preserved separately from interpretation.
Evidence package clearly distinguishes direct screen evidence from chat-reported interpretation.

validation_checks: System error area captured
AGV-related status area captured
Any visible error strings preserved exactly as shown
escalation_conditions: If AGVs are not moving and no explicit AGV fault is shown
If system errors suggest missing AGV or control object issues
If HMI symptoms conflict with service/API status

title: Proc 228086 03
operational_intent: Collect screenshot-backed service responsiveness evidence before or after restart-oriented actions.

steps.validation_check: The screenshot clearly shows the request being used for service responsiveness validation.
A response is captured and its status is recorded; if no response, that is documented as failure evidence.
Service state is captured in the same evidence set as the API result.
The note distinguishes service responsiveness from end-to-end production recovery.

validation_checks: GetAgvStatuses response captured
HTTP status recorded if visible
OptiSweep service state captured in Services console
escalation_conditions: If API gives no response
If service is not running
If API succeeds but AGVs still do not move

title: Proc 228086 04
operational_intent: Document the candidate recovery sequence exactly as supported by the case evidence, while marking missing execution details and approval boundaries.

steps.validation_check: No restart step proceeds until estop coordination is documented.
Command syntax and path are captured well enough for SME review before reuse.
Gateway page is reachable before proceeding to API validation.
The result is explicitly recorded as either responsive or non-responsive.
A repeated API check is performed after the OptiSweep restart action.
Release is documented after, not before, the confirmation step.

validation_checks: Estop applied before restart actions
Ignition restart command captured or documented
API response checked after restart
Estop released only after confirmation
escalation_conditions: If restart authority is unclear
If Ignition does not return after command
If GetAgvStatuses still gives no response after restart
If onsite coordination is not established

title: Proc 223554 01
operational_intent: Preserve a source-backed incident timeline and escalation record before deeper troubleshooting or handoff.

steps.validation_check: Case number and subject match the incident package and the screenshot clearly shows the case creation/update card.
Work order ID and entered time are readable and align with the incident timeline summary.
Timestamp and presence of the Teams bridge are readable from the screenshot.
Recovery comments are captured as validation signals only and not converted into root-cause statements.
Resolution wording is preserved verbatim and flagged as documented action rather than validated cause.

validation_checks: Case number, subject, timestamps, and work order ID are captured from visible source records
Recovery timestamps are separated from cause assumptions
escalation_conditions: CBRE work order shows P1 emergency
Teams bridge or onsite dispatch is required
Case wording indicates server/service issue with production impact

title: Proc 223554 02
operational_intent: Narrow the issue to AGV/tote state inconsistency using source-backed observations before escalation or recovery actions.

steps.validation_check: Symptom wording is captured exactly and separated from later diagnosis.
The statement is labeled candidate-only and not treated as validated root cause.
Each AGV ID is tied to the exact wording from the source and not normalized beyond AGV formatting.
Only fields that are visually legible are recorded; noisy OCR fields are left blank or marked uncertain.
Escalation note references source artifacts and does not claim confirmed cause.

validation_checks: Specific AGV IDs and mismatch statements are captured from source
Visual robot detail evidence is treated as supportive, not definitive
escalation_conditions: Onsite confirmation is needed
AGV location/state cannot be reconciled from available evidence
Issue persists after basic state review

title: Proc 223554 03
operational_intent: Obtain actionable onsite observations and preserve them for support coordination during an active disruption.

steps.validation_check: Onsite status and site-down wording are captured from the same coherent message block.
Requested checks and returned observations are both captured with timestamps.
E-stop remains marked as considered unless explicit execution evidence is present.
Recovery indicators are captured separately from the actions that may have preceded them.
Summary references source artifacts and clearly marks unknowns such as whether E-stop occurred.

validation_checks: Onsite contact, location, and requested checks are documented
Heartbeat/lost-connection observations are preserved with timestamps
escalation_conditions: No one is onsite
Site is reported down
Heartbeat or connection issue is suspected but not confirmed remotely

title: Proc 223554 04
operational_intent: Preserve a reusable evidence-backed recovery pattern for SME review and future controlled documentation.

steps.validation_check: Summary is captured verbatim and explicitly marked as candidate operational evidence.
Only AGV removals explicitly stated in the source are recorded.
No endpoint URLs, payloads, or credentials are invented; only visible action descriptions are recorded.
Recovery confirmation is separated from inferred root cause.
Procedure remains candidate-only and blocked from promotion until SME approval and role boundaries are defined.
Service-reset wording is preserved separately from the Teams manual-action summary.

validation_checks: Recovery actions are captured as documented actions only
No unsupported API endpoint or execution details are invented
escalation_conditions: Manual API intervention is being considered
Support team lacks approved instructions
Issue requires advanced recovery beyond standard triage

title: Proc 229716 01
operational_intent: Restore AGV-to-tipper flow after service-related stoppage symptoms while preserving evidence and validating recovery.

steps.validation_check: Restart target is explicitly Optisweep and symptoms align with this incident pattern.
E-stop instruction is acknowledged and restart does not begin until controlled stop is in place.
Correct service entry is identified before any restart action is taken.
Service restart completes without switching to an unintended service such as Ignition OPC.
E-stop is removed only after restart completion and site is ready for movement validation.
Both AGV movement and hospital tote removal are restored, or remaining issue is explicitly documented for escalation.

validation_checks: AGVs are moving again
Hospital tote removal works after recovery
System can be declared operational by site/support
escalation_conditions: If AGVs do not resume movement after restart
If hospital tote removal still fails after restart
If heartbeat timeout symptoms persist
If service restart cannot be performed due to permissions or unclear service state

title: Proc 229716 02
operational_intent: Preserve screenshot-backed diagnostic evidence showing heartbeat timeout context without over-asserting noisy OCR details.

steps.validation_check: The screenshot clearly identifies the tipper heartbeat/alarm context rather than an unrelated screen.
At least one screenshot preserves the message area and timestamps well enough for manual review.
Screenshot is sufficient to support manual comparison with the written note 'no RMS faults' without over-claiming exact values.
Written summary and screenshots are linked together so later reviewers know the screenshots are supporting evidence, not sole proof.

validation_checks: Screenshots clearly show the relevant view names or context
Capture includes timestamps/messages if visible
Evidence is linked to the incident record
escalation_conditions: If heartbeat/alarm views cannot be accessed
If screenshots are too unclear to preserve message/timestamp context
If visual evidence conflicts with operator-reported status

title: Proc 229716 03
operational_intent: Preserve server-side evidence needed for later engineering review, especially when service restart restores operation before root cause is validated.

steps.validation_check: Collected evidence list is explicitly separated into Windows/server event logs, Ignition logs, and unresolved DB logs.
Collector is on the correct server before exporting logs.
Windows/server event logs for the incident window are saved and their location is recorded.
Ignition log files are saved and can be associated with the incident window.
DB evidence is either collected with timestamps/error text preserved, or the collection gap is explicitly recorded for escalation.
Evidence location and filenames are documented so another reviewer can retrieve the files.

validation_checks: Ignition logs are saved
Windows event logs are saved
Any database-log gap is explicitly documented if collection cannot be completed
Saved evidence location is recorded
escalation_conditions: If DB logs are requested but support cannot locate or transfer them
If server event or Ignition logs cannot be exported
If evidence suggests database timeout but no DB-specific artifacts can be collected

title: Proc 229716 04
operational_intent: Verify that service restart restored the operational functions affected during the incident.

steps.validation_check: AGV movement is confirmed before proceeding to final closure.
Hospital tote removal succeeds after the test or after relogin.
Named site contact confirms both AGV and hospital functions are restored.
Operational status is documented only after both validation checks are complete.

validation_checks: AGVs are moving
Hospital tote removal succeeds
Site contact confirms proper AGV operation and hospital function
escalation_conditions: If AGVs still do not move after restart
If hospital tote removal still fails after relogin
If site contact cannot confirm proper operation

title: Proc 229488 01
operational_intent: Restore production quickly while preserving traceable evidence and escalation context around the restart action.

steps.validation_check: Repeat-incident context and production impact are both visible in source-backed screens before restart coordination proceeds.
Escalation and urgency evidence is preserved before the restart is executed.
A source-backed indication exists that restart was coordinated with site state, even if exact safety checklist is not visible.
Teams or case notes confirm the restart occurred at the recorded time.
At least one source-backed recovery confirmation is visible after the restart.
Case record shows recovery documented without claiming validated root cause.

validation_checks: Site reports startup progressing after restart.
Production resumes or site is operating again.
Case notes reflect restart timing and outcome.
escalation_conditions: No authorized remote access available.
Restart does not restore production promptly.
Repeated recurrence after prior restart.
Need for root cause confirmation beyond service recovery.

title: Proc 229488 02
operational_intent: Produce a reviewable evidence package that separates database-timeout claims from visible OPC UA, alarm, and reconnect evidence.

steps.validation_check: The timeout statement is preserved as discussion context and not mislabeled as direct log evidence.
Each screenshot includes enough of the table to preserve logger, time, and message together.
Only mappings explicitly stated in source are recorded; unknown endpoints remain unknown.
Alarm screenshots preserve both the timestamp and the exact alarm wording for the fault window.
The captured log clearly shows reconnect/rebuild behavior after fault conditions.
A direct timeout artifact is captured, or the absence of direct timeout evidence is explicitly documented as a gap.
Post-change monitoring evidence is preserved separately from the original outage evidence.

validation_checks: Each captured screen includes timestamp, logger/source, and endpoint or error text where visible.
Database timeout evidence is kept separate from OPC UA reset evidence unless the same screen explicitly links them.
At least one post-fault and one follow-up monitoring screen are preserved.
escalation_conditions: No direct database timeout evidence can be located on the WCS server.
Repeated resets involve infrastructure-owned endpoints.
Need engineering review of subscription rebuild behavior.

title: Proc 229488 03
operational_intent: Rapidly collect the minimum high-value triage signals needed to decide whether to escalate, gather deeper logs, or pursue recovery action.

steps.validation_check: A current tipper-state answer is captured rather than assumed.
Heartbeat evidence is captured as a value or screen, not just inferred from AGV stoppage.
HMI error state is explicitly checked and documented.
Manual workaround evidence is captured because it indicates active operational impact.
Status screen captures endpoint names and states together in one view.
At least one recent disconnect/reconnect sequence is captured if communications issues are suspected.

validation_checks: Tipper state, heartbeat state, and HMI error state are all explicitly checked.
Endpoint status or disconnect evidence is captured if communications issues are suspected.
escalation_conditions: Heartbeat appears abnormal and AGVs remain stopped.
No HMI error but endpoint disconnect evidence is present.
Multiple endpoints show resets or disabled states.
Site reports unsafe conditions or manual clearing is underway.

title: Proc 229777 01
operational_intent: Confirm and document whether a tipper heartbeat failure is present during an OptiSweep/AGV startup stoppage and preserve source-backed evidence for escalation or recovery coordination.

steps.validation_check: At least one source-backed symptom matches the startup stoppage pattern before proceeding.
Engineer ownership and active diagnostic session are explicitly documented.
A visible failed heartbeat indicator or equivalent status text is captured, or the absence of such evidence is explicitly noted.
Prior case reference is recorded as similarity context, not as validated cause.

validation_checks: Heartbeat failure observation is documented in case notes or visible system view
Symptoms align with startup stoppage and no-flow-to-tippers condition
Evidence is preserved with timestamps and case linkage
escalation_conditions: Heartbeat failure cannot be confirmed from available views
Condition recurs after restart/reset
Need to compare against prior similar case or deeper controls/infrastructure review

title: Proc 229777 02
operational_intent: Restore system operation after the observed startup stoppage using the same coordinated actions documented in the case, while preserving evidence and role boundaries.

steps.validation_check: There is a documented basis for attempting restart/reset and a named site contact is engaged.
Master e-stop application is explicitly confirmed before restart proceeds.
Restart action is documented with time and operator identity.
Tipper pause/unpause action is documented as completed and associated with the recovery attempt.
Both operational recovery and customer acceptance are documented before closure.

validation_checks: Restart and tipper reset actions are documented
System returns to back-up-and-running state
Customer/site confirms restoration
escalation_conditions: Recovery does not restore flow
Heartbeat or no-path condition persists after restart
Site cannot safely perform required coordination

title: Proc 229777 03
operational_intent: Preserve the specific follow-on diagnostic evidence types discussed in the case so recurring AGV connectivity issues can be investigated without inventing unsupported log paths or tooling details.

steps.validation_check: At least one robot identifier or symptom/time window is recorded before requesting logs.
Disconnect evidence includes robot identifiers and timestamps, not just a verbal summary.
Athena/RMS-equivalent evidence is clearly labeled so it is not confused with AGV-side logs.
AGV-side logging request is tied to specific robots and recurrence goals, not a generic request.
Evidence package clearly distinguishes RMS/Athena data from AGV-side logs and states whether any requested logs were unavailable.

validation_checks: Requested log sources are identified by name
Robot identifiers and time windows are recorded
Collected evidence is tied to a specific symptom or recurrence window
escalation_conditions: RMS/Athena data is unavailable
AGV-side logs require vendor/on-site setup
Disconnect pattern remains unclear after initial evidence review

title: Proc 229777 04
operational_intent: Document and preserve the minimum source-backed steps for arranging local OT network access for diagnostics without inventing unsupported network configuration procedures.

steps.validation_check: There is explicit source-backed justification for local OT network access.
The connection point matches the location described in the support evidence before any device is connected.
Static IP requirement and target IP are recorded exactly from source before connection testing.
Connection result is documented with timestamp and target system identity.

validation_checks: Correct OT network port is identified
Static IP requirement is documented
Target RMS IP or destination is recorded
escalation_conditions: Personnel cannot connect locally
Static IP assignment is not authorized or fails
Target system remains unreachable after local connection attempt
